task_completion.description: Read the Fire Strategy Brief on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer is open displaying the contents of 'fire_strategy_brief.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Create at least two IfcZone entities for fire compartments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM/Bonsai interface (e.g., Outliner, Spatial Manager, or Group Manager) shows at least two newly created IfcZone entities (e.g., 'Ground Floor Compartment', 'First Floor Compartment').

task_completion.description: Assign existing IfcSpace entities to the created IfcZones based on their storey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM interface shows IfcSpace entities nested under, linked to, or grouped within the newly created IfcZone entities in the spatial tree or group assignment panels.

task_completion.description: Add a fire resistance property to at least one of the zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties panel in BlenderBIM for one of the IfcZone entities displays a custom property set or property name containing the word 'fire' (e.g., 'Pset_ZoneFireSafety' or 'FireRating').

task_completion.description: Save the enriched model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file export dialog in BlenderBIM or a file manager/terminal window showing the file 'fzk_fire_compartments.ifc' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the BlenderBIM/Bonsai GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of Blender's Outliner, Properties panel, and Spatial Manager, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: The fire resistance property was genuinely added within the BIM software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The property is visible in the BlenderBIM Object Properties UI, not just typed into a text file or mocked in a terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the software by using a Python script to directly modify the IFC file in the background.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows step-by-step manual creation of zones, assignment of spaces, and property editing in the Blender interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The authentic FZK-Haus IFC model contains exactly 7 IfcSpace entities (6 on Erdgeschoss, 1 on Dachgeschoss). The agent must create exactly 2 IfcZone entities and assign these spaces to them based on their storey. The fire resistance property must contain the word 'fire' (case-insensitive) in its property set or property name. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fire_compartments.ifc.
